creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
This is one of those things for which the C99 rationale V5.10 is
clearer than reading the standard and figuring out implications
in some respects:

QUOTE
The va_list type is not necessarily assignable, however a
function can pass a pointer to its initialized argument list
object object as noted below. The wording has been changed
in C99 to state clearly that va_list is an object type.
END QUOTE
